student_names: Abdullah Mohamed Shosha, Ahmed Mohamed Abdulfatah, Eslam Saudi Saad, Mohamed Mohsen Elbana, Yousef Alaa Mohamed Ibrahim
abstract: This document has been prepared by a group of Computer Science students, Faculty of computers and Artificial Intelligence at Benha University to help Users or students who will play our Game. Using Augmented Reality and Unity Techniques in this Game. The state of the art in Augmented Reality (AR) games. Distinguished advancements in terms of entertainment and serious games from both the research and industry are presented. These works are then analyzed across metrics like technology usage, game genre and chronology. Via this analysis, trends are extracted and novel insights into promising domains are eventually concluded, in both the perspectives of research and commercial development. So Our Game will take the Users or students who will play the Game to an adventure to find and Collect the Treasure of The Ancient Egyptians and know more information about it. So Our player should go around and navigate to search for Treasure that we add to some of the most famous places like the Egyptian Museum or the Pyramids of Giza or somewhere. After the player collects a certain number of treasure, according to the level in which he is, Then he can move to the next level of the game.
description: Treasure Hunt is a location-based mobile augmented reality game designed to educate users about Egyptian history. Players navigate real-world historical sites, using GPS and AR technology to discover and collect virtual 3D treasures. The game aims to promote tourism and provide an engaging, interactive learning experience about ancient Egyptian culture.
problem_statement: The project addresses the technological gap in AR accessibility, specifically the lack of AR support on many Android devices compared to iOS, and the high cost of specialized AR hardware. Additionally, it tackles the challenge of creating user-friendly, location-based AR experiences that are accessible on standard smartphones.
proposed_solution: The team developed a location-based AR game using Unity that utilizes standard smartphone sensors (GPS, gyroscope, magnetic sensor) to create an immersive experience. The solution focuses on optimizing AR performance for mobile devices to make the technology accessible to a wider audience without requiring expensive AR gear.
objectives:
1. Improve system performance and reliability.
2. Provide efficient data analysis and reporting features.
3. Enhance user experience and usability.
4. Create a mobile-friendly and accessible application.
5. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
6. Improve decision-making using artificial intelligence techniques.
7. Implement intelligent AI-based functionalities.

project_title: Treatment Child With Cerebral Palsy using VR supplement
student_names: Mohamed Ahmed Imam Talab, Mohamed Ashraf Fahmy, Mohamed Bashir Antar Bedir, Mohamed Ahmed Nabih El-Dighidy, Kyrillos Samir Halim, Latifa Mohamed El-Bakry, Manar Samir Mohamed El-Absy
abstract: This project aims to explore the effectiveness of utilizing virtual reality (VR) technology in the treatment of children with cerebral palsy (CP). Specifically, we developed two mini-sport games within 3D Unity environment and computer vision: football and squash. The football game targets various physical aspects including balance, hip flexors, knee extensors, ankle dorsiflexors, as well as addressing symptoms such as ataxia and polyneuropathy commonly associated with CP. On the other hand, Tennise focuses on enhancing fine motor skills and occupational therapy for these children. Through immersive VR experiences tailored to the needs of children with CP, we seek to provide an engaging and effective therapeutic approach to improve their physical and cognitive abilities. This study highlights the potential of VR-based interventions in pediatric rehabilitation, offering a promising avenue for enhancing the quality of life and functional independence of children with CP.
description: The project is a VR-based therapeutic system designed to assist children with Cerebral Palsy in their physical rehabilitation. By gamifying physiotherapy through immersive sports simulations like football and squash, the system encourages children to perform necessary exercises to improve motor skills, balance, and muscle control in an engaging and motivating environment.
problem_statement: Traditional therapy for Cerebral Palsy often suffers from limited accessibility, lack of personalization, high costs, and difficulty in tracking patient progress, which can lead to suboptimal outcomes and decreased patient motivation.
proposed_solution: The project proposes an immersive VR gaming system that provides customized, interactive exercises targeting specific muscle groups and motor skills, making therapy more enjoyable and accessible for children with CP.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Enhance user experience and usability.
3. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
4. Support future scalability and feature expansion.
5. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.
6. Enable real-time monitoring using connected devices.
7. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.

project_title: UNI-ASSIST: An intelligent System for Assessing Student Participation and Interaction
student_names: Mariam Anwer Mohamed Youssef, Eman Saeed Mohamed Mahmoud, Hagar Ashraf Mohamed, Mahmoud Ahmed Ahmed Araby, Mark Naiem Zakher Mosaad, Youssef Emad Gamal, Omar Mohamed Abdelrahman, Radwa Mohamed Khalil, Alaa Essam Abdelaal
abstract: UNI-ASSIST is an intelligent system designed to modernize the traditional, paper-based student attendance and academic communication process. By utilizing QR code technology for secure and efficient attendance tracking, the system eliminates manual errors and time-consuming administrative tasks. Additionally, it provides a centralized platform for faculty to manage course materials and communicate important updates, serving as a more structured and reliable alternative to informal social media groups.
description: UNI-ASSIST is a comprehensive academic management platform that integrates three core functionalities: a secure QR-code-based attendance system, an automated academic material management system, and a dedicated communication portal for faculty-student interaction. The system aims to digitize the educational environment, ensuring accurate attendance records, streamlined access to course content, and improved information dissemination.
problem_statement: Traditional attendance systems rely on manual paper-based methods that are inefficient, prone to falsification, and time-consuming. Furthermore, communication between faculty and students is often fragmented across social media, leading to information loss, difficulty in accessing course materials, and general administrative confusion.
proposed_solution: The project proposes an automated system that uses time-sensitive QR codes for attendance to prevent fraud and save time. It also includes a centralized module for faculty to upload course materials and post official announcements, ensuring that all students have reliable access to critical academic information.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Improve tracking accuracy and operational visibility.
3. Enhance user experience and usability.
4. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
5. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
6. Improve system performance and reliability.
7. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
8. Improve workflow and management efficiency.

abstract: This project introduces a comprehensive digital platform for the Universal Health Insurance system. It aims to streamline healthcare services, reduce administrative burdens, and ensure equitable access to medical care for all citizens, including state employees, students, and the unemployed. By transitioning from manual, fragmented processes to an integrated digital solution, the system improves transparency, efficiency, and the quality of health services provided to the public.
description: The project is a web-based information system designed to support the implementation of a national Universal Health Insurance program. It addresses current inefficiencies in healthcare administration by providing a centralized platform for citizens to access services, manage insurance records, and interact with healthcare providers. The system is built to handle complex insurance workflows, including family-based coverage, diagnostic services, and treatment tracking.
problem_statement: Current health insurance systems are fragmented, lack transparency, and often fail to cover all segments of society. Issues include high administrative costs, corruption risks, lack of effective control mechanisms, and difficulty for citizens to access services efficiently, leading to increased financial and health burdens on individuals.
proposed_solution: The development of a comprehensive, centralized web-based health insurance portal that digitizes the insurance process. This system provides a unified interface for citizens to manage their health insurance, ensures equitable distribution of resources, and implements digital controls to prevent fraud and improve service delivery efficiency.
objectives:
1. Develop a responsive and user-friendly web application.
2. Improve tracking accuracy and operational visibility.
3. Enhance user experience and usability.
4. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
5. Support future scalability and feature expansion.

student_names: Mohamed Adel El-Sayed, Belal Ahmed Ali, Mohamed Adel Mohamed, Sayed Fotoh El-Sayed, Yousef Ali Ahmed, Mohamed Mortada Abdirashid, Mohamed Gamal El Sayed, Mostafa Ali Mostafa
abstract: In the contemporary landscape of higher education, students face numerous challenges in navigating their academic journey effectively. To address these challenges and enhance the student experience, we introduce "University Companion, " an innovative application designed to support students in managing their academic endeavors seamlessly. University Companion offers a range of features aimed at streamlining the academic experience, including a comprehensive course catalog, GPA tracking functionality, personalized advice based on academic performance, and an integrated chat-bot for real-time assistance. Leveraging advanced algorithms and natural language processing (NLP) technology, the application provides students with actionable insights, empowering them to make informed decisions and maximize their academic potential. Moreover, University Companion fosters a sense of community and collaboration among students by providing various community groups tailored to each grade level and department. These groups serve as platforms for students to connect, share resources, seek help from peers, and engage in private chats, akin to popular messaging applications like WhatsApp or Messenger. Furthermore, University Companion integrates professors into the platform, allowing them to log in with their academic accounts to add courses, make announcements, and engage with students directly. Professors can seamlessly contribute to the academic ecosystem by uploading course materials, providing timely updates, and fostering communication between students and faculty members. Through its intuitive interface, user-centric design, commitment to fostering community engagement, and integration of professors, University Companion aims to revolutionize the way students engage with their studies. By facilitating efficient course selection, GPA monitoring, personalized advice, community collaboration, private chats, and direct interaction with professors, the application seeks to empower students, foster academic success, and promote a culture of lifelong learning within the university community.
description: University Companion is a centralized digital platform and application designed to streamline the academic experience for university students. It integrates course management, resource sharing, community collaboration, and an AI-powered chatbot to provide real-time academic assistance and personalized guidance.
problem_statement: University students face significant challenges in managing and accessing course materials, reference books, and academic resources spread across disparate platforms. Additionally, they often struggle with navigating academic requirements and obtaining instant clarification on course content, leading to delays in understanding and academic progress.
proposed_solution: The project provides a centralized hub called 'University Companion' that consolidates academic resources into a single, accessible interface. It features an AI-driven chatbot utilizing Natural Language Processing (NLP) to provide real-time support, alongside community features for student-to-student and student-to-professor interaction.
objectives:
1. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
2. Improve decision-making using artificial intelligence techniques.
3. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
4. Improve system performance and reliability.
5. Implement intelligent AI-based functionalities.

project_title: Vehicle Movement tracking system using IoT
student_names: Aya Moustafa Mohamed, Karima Refat Younis, Basma El-saeed Mohamed, Aya Mohamed Ashoor
abstract: A vehicle tracking system is one of the most important things in real life. A vehicle tracking system will design and implement for tracking the movement of any equipped vehicle from any location at any time. The system uses a popular technology that combines a Smartphone application with a microcontroller. This will be an inexpensive solution compared to others. The design of the system can be implemented using ESP technology that is one of the most common ways for vehicle tracking. The object is tracked in real-time. The vehicle tracking system uses the GPS module to get geographic coordinates (longitudes and latitudes) at regular time intervals. A Smartphone application is also developed for concurrent monitoring the vehicle location. The Google Maps API is used to display the vehicle using Smartphone application. Thus, users will be able to monitor a moving vehicle on demand using the Smartphone application and they determine the estimated distance and time for the vehicle to arrive at a given destination.
description: This project presents a low-cost, real-time vehicle tracking system designed to monitor the location of moving or stationary vehicles. By integrating GPS modules with ESP Wi-Fi microcontrollers, the system captures precise geographic coordinates and transmits them to a central server. Users can then view the vehicle's real-time position on a smartphone application integrated with Google Maps, providing enhanced security and logistical oversight.
problem_statement: The increasing number of road accidents, vehicle thefts, and the need for monitoring the safety of individuals (such as children in school buses) create a critical demand for reliable, real-time vehicle tracking solutions to ensure safety and rapid response.
proposed_solution: The proposed solution is an IoT-based tracking system that utilizes a GPS module for location acquisition and an ESP Wi-Fi module for data transmission. The system processes these coordinates and displays them on a user-friendly smartphone application using the Google Maps API, allowing for real-time monitoring of vehicle movement.
objectives:
1. Enhance system security and protect sensitive user data.
2. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
3. Support future scalability and feature expansion.
4. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
5. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
6. Integrate IoT technologies for smart automation.
7. Enable real-time monitoring using connected devices.
8. Enable real-time monitoring and tracking capabilities.

project_title: Vibrant: IOT Based Smart Monitoring System For Intensive Care Utilization
student_names: Mahmoud Tarek Mohamed, Asmaa Mosad Mohamed, Abeer Kamal Ali, Eman AbdElwanees Hamad, Abdel Rahman Baiuomy Metwaly, Mahmoud Rawy Nady
abstract: In Egypt, citizens suffer to obtain medical services, particularly intensive care due to the shortage of the intensive care units. One of the reasons for the late arrival is the shortage of information about the available rooms for intensive care in hospitals. So patients move from hospital to another unit they find a room. We tried to solve a part of this problem by carrying out a system to provide the information about the utilization of intensive care rooms at hospitals in the region. This system can help citizens to find out the nearest available room to move to directly. As a result, the survival rate will increase and the cost of treatment will decrease significantly. Thereby the efficiency of intensive care utilization raises. The main idea is to provide the information about intensive care utilization at hospitals based on Internet to Things (IOT) technology. The System is divided into three units: device node, gateway node, and database server. The device node watches the intensive care unit. The Gateway nodes collect the information from device nodes and send it to the server to collect the data and make it available for citizens. The project aims at designing, implementing and deploying a cost effective IOT system to monitor the intensive care utilization and give citizens access to this information. Hence, citizens can move to the nearest available hospital directly at the right time.
description: Vibrant is an IoT-based system designed to monitor the availability and utilization of intensive care units (ICU) in hospitals. The system consists of device nodes installed in ICU rooms, gateway nodes that aggregate data, a central database server, and a mobile application that provides real-time information to citizens, doctors, and ambulance drivers.
problem_statement: Patients in Egypt often face critical delays in receiving intensive care due to a lack of real-time information regarding bed availability. Current methods, such as calling hospitals, are inefficient, inaccurate, and lead to patients being transported between multiple hospitals before finding an available unit, which negatively impacts survival rates.
proposed_solution: The project proposes an IoT-based monitoring system that tracks ICU bed occupancy in real-time. By deploying sensor-based device nodes in hospitals and connecting them through gateways to a central server, the system provides an accessible interface (mobile application) for users to locate the nearest hospital with an available ICU bed, thereby reducing travel time and improving emergency response efficiency.
objectives:
1. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
2. Create a mobile-friendly and accessible application.
3. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.
4. Support future scalability and feature expansion.
5. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.

abstract: Virtual reality and simulation is a breaking technology that applied in many fields (Educational, Medical, Industrial, etc.), and many applications had appeared in the last few years that were very helpful, and success especially in the educational field. So we had the challenge to make a useful, success, and attractive virtual application to be our graduation project. And we choose the logic field because it will be helpful for the students in our faculty, and in many other facilities. Also logic is just perfect field to be simulated. So we choose making a virtual logic laboratory. This document describes the work completed for the final year project, design of an efficient, helpful virtual logic lab, all the work we had done until we had finished this project. This documentation includes an introduction about our project, research results, and a great background about simulation, and virtual reality. This document also includes the steps of implementing our projects by java programming, and how we had examined to ensure that it works correctly. This is a documented step by step to help the reader to understand what is our project, and how does it work.
description: Virtual Logic Lab is an interactive software application designed to simulate digital logic circuits. It allows students to connect various logic gates, specify inputs, and observe the resulting outputs in a virtual environment, effectively replacing the need for physical laboratory equipment.
problem_statement: Traditional logic labs suffer from issues such as equipment malfunction, limited availability of tools, and time constraints. Students often struggle with limited access to physical labs and the potential for damaging expensive components during experiments.
proposed_solution: The Virtual Logic Lab provides a safe, cost-effective, and accessible software-based simulation environment. It allows users to build and test logic circuits without the risk of hardware damage, providing features like truth table generation, timing diagrams, and error detection.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Enhance user experience and usability.
3. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
4. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
5. Improve learning experience and educational accessibility.

student_names: Marwan Osama Elsafy, Omar Toson Khater, Moamen Ali Hassan, Ali Samy Abo Elngah, Eman Saeed Madbouly
abstract: Our idea is to design and develop a virtual reality application that aims to help children with cancer control their pain during chemotherapy session, and not feeling long in the session. Our virtual reality application takes the patient from a real world to a virtual world using two level of the game. Our virtual reality application is divided into two levels; in the 1st level the patient use a gun to shot specific dinosaur that have a color and size which will be determined for him at the beginning of the game. The patient must shot all specific dinosaurs to win this level and be able to go to the next level. But in case of lose he should repeat this level again. In the 2nd level the patient use a gun to shot a specific dinosaur, but in this level the dinosaurs attack the patient and a certain number must be killed to win the game.
description: This project is a VR-based therapeutic game designed to distract pediatric cancer patients during chemotherapy sessions. By immersing the child in a 3D interactive environment, the application aims to reduce the perception of pain and anxiety associated with medical treatments, providing a non-pharmacological method for pain management.
problem_statement: Children undergoing chemotherapy experience severe pain and emotional distress. Traditional methods of pain management, such as medication, often have side effects and lose effectiveness over time. Passive distractions like watching movies are often insufficient because they do not fully immerse the child or separate them from their immediate clinical environment.
proposed_solution: A VR application that provides an immersive, interactive gaming experience. The game uses two distinct levels—one focused on target identification and another on defensive gameplay—to engage the child's senses and divert their attention away from the painful medical procedure.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
3. Implement intelligent AI-based functionalities.
4. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.
5. Improve workflow and management efficiency.
6. Improve decision-making using artificial intelligence techniques.
7. Enhance user experience and usability.
8. Support future scalability and feature expansion.

abstract: The project introduces a virtual store system that replaces physical product displays with boards containing product images and QR codes. Customers use their smartphones to scan these codes, review product details, and place orders, which are then routed to the nearest branch for fulfillment. The system aims to reduce the overhead costs of physical retail space while providing a convenient, anytime-anywhere shopping experience for customers.
description: This project proposes a mobile-based virtual shopping platform that allows retailers to establish a presence in high-traffic areas like subway stations or malls without the need for full-scale physical stores. By utilizing QR codes on product boards, customers can browse and purchase items directly from their mobile devices. The backend system manages order routing to the nearest distribution branch, optimizing inventory and delivery processes.
problem_statement: Investors face high costs for physical store construction, land acquisition, and maintenance, while customers often struggle with limited time and the inconvenience of traditional shopping, including travel and waiting times.
proposed_solution: A virtual store system that uses QR codes to represent products, allowing customers to shop via mobile devices. The solution includes a distributed server architecture that routes orders to the nearest branch, enabling retailers to expand their reach with minimal physical infrastructure.

abstract: Imagine being able to explore the wonders of Egypt like the Pyramids of Giza, the temples of Luxor, or the bustling streets of Cairo without leaving your home. This project is a virtual tour platform designed for people who love history, culture, and adventure but face challenges like safety concerns, high travel costs, or difficulty adapting to unfamiliar environments. The platform is organized by governorates and uses 3D and 360-degree technology to provide an immersive experience. It also features an AI voice-over for historical context and personalized recommendations based on user interests, budget, and time, while fostering a social community through user reviews.
description: The Voyage System is a comprehensive web-based virtual tourism platform that brings Egypt's landmarks to a global audience. It serves as a digital passport, allowing users to explore various regions through immersive 3D and 360-degree environments. The system integrates AI to provide personalized travel suggestions and educational voice-overs, while also incorporating community features like user ratings and reviews to enhance trust and social engagement.
problem_statement: Many people are unable to visit Egypt due to safety concerns, high travel costs, time constraints, lack of reliable information, and the difficulty of navigating unfamiliar environments.
proposed_solution: A virtual tour platform that provides safe, affordable, and accessible exploration of Egypt from home. It offers 3D/360-degree immersive tours, AI-driven personalized recommendations, and reliable historical information, effectively acting as a personal tour guide and travel planner.

abstract: V-Traker application is an android and Arduino based application that provides the users with services that they might need during their life, either for saving time, saving efforts or security. This application benefits from the services that provided by smart phones and Arduino such as Bluetooth, Vibration, ...etc.
description: Did you know that the average person spends 10 minutes a day looking for things they own but cannot find. The idea is that you attach something to whatever is important to you such as a handbag or your keys and register the object with an android application on your smart phone using Bluetooth. Once registered, whenever you open the app (or go out of range of the object) you'll be able to track where it was and receive an alert with the last known location. There are lots of different ways to do that tracking and different form factors of the device so we've rounded the latest up for you to make an easier decision. After the jump we've got a look at the options available and which ones have the best features. This issue and the fact that smart phones became very common, made us thinking to find a good and an easy way to help the people and make it easier to find their lost stuff than before, and from here we got the idea of our graduation project which is “V-Traker”
problem_statement: It always seems like you’re losing your wallet or keys, right? Valuables like keys, wallet, pets...etc. are able to be lost or stolen and it's difficult to find it easily.
proposed_solution: V-Traker is a device uses Bluetooth to communicate with the companion android application on your smartphone. The V-Traker app discovers a V-Traker device and establishes a connection to it using the Bluetooth signal.
objectives:
1. Enable real-time monitoring and tracking capabilities.
2. Improve tracking accuracy and operational visibility.
3. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
4. Improve system performance and reliability.
5. Support future scalability and feature expansion.

student_names: Ali Elsayed Ragab, Manar Abdulrahman Ahmed, Mohab Mustafa Fathy, Mohammed Ehab Hassan, Yomna Mohammed Abdelfatah
abstract: In the rapidly evolving landscape of cybersecurity, organizations face persistent threats and vulnerabilities that pose significant risks to their sensitive information and critical systems. To address these challenges, this graduation project introduces a state-of-the-art Vulnerability Assessment Tool (VAT) designed to provide a comprehensive and efficient approach to identify, analyze, and mitigate potential security weaknesses within an organization's infrastructure. The Vulnerability Assessment Tool project is a strategic initiative aimed at developing a robust software solution to fortify organizational defenses against cyber threats. In an era where digital assets face incessant risks, this project seeks to empower security professionals and IT administrators with an advanced tool capable of proactively identifying, assessing, and prioritizing vulnerabilities within infrastructures.
description: This project presents an intelligent surveillance and monitoring system designed to enhance public safety through real-time weapon detection and analysis. The system utilizes advanced deep learning and computer vision techniques, specifically the Faster R-CNN algorithm combined with the Inception V2 architecture, to accurately identify firearms and cold weapons from surveillance camera feeds. Once a weapon is detected, the platform automatically triggers alerts to notify security personnel, enabling rapid intervention and threat prevention. In addition to detection capabilities, the system provides detailed information about commonly used weapons, including firing range, rate of fire, and damage level, supporting security teams, law enforcement agencies, and forensic investigators in criminal analysis and decision-making. By integrating AI-powered detection with intelligent monitoring and data analysis features, the system aims to improve surveillance efficiency, strengthen security operations, and reduce the risks associated with violent incidents and attacks.
problem_statement: Manual vulnerability assessment processes are labor-intensive, time-consuming, and prone to human error, leading to gaps in security coverage. Furthermore, the rapidly evolving threat landscape makes it difficult for organizations to keep up with new vulnerabilities, leaving them exposed to potential cyber-attacks.
proposed_solution: The project proposes a comprehensive Vulnerability Assessment Tool (VAT) designed to automatically scan networks, systems, and applications for security weaknesses and misconfigurations. It generates detailed reports highlighting potential vulnerabilities and provides actionable mitigation steps to enhance the overall cybersecurity posture and prevent potential cyber attacks.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Implement secure authentication and authorization mechanisms.
3. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
4. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
5. Enhance user experience and usability.

abstract: Wasally is a lifestyle e-commerce android application which retails various fashion and lifestyle products. It enables registered users to purchase products instantly using PayPal or Cash on Delivery. The system provides administrators and managers with tools to view orders and manage the shopping process, aiming to save consumer time and effort through a multi-tiered architecture.
description: Wasally is an online shopping platform designed for mobile devices. It features four distinct user roles: Visitor (browsing), User (purchasing), Shop Owner (product management), and Delivery Boy (order fulfillment). The system focuses on providing a seamless shopping experience with integrated payment options and efficient local delivery.
problem_statement: The project addresses several e-commerce challenges: poor product quality (addressed via feedback/ratings), sizing issues (addressed by providing detailed dimensions), limited payment methods (addressed by adding Cash on Delivery), and slow delivery times (addressed by a direct shop-to-consumer delivery model).
proposed_solution: A mobile-based e-commerce application that connects local shops directly to consumers. It implements a robust backend using PHP and a relational database, providing a user-friendly interface for browsing, secure payment processing, and a streamlined delivery management system.

project_title: Weapon Detection System for Smart Surveillance
student_names: Abdel-lateef Eid Abdel-lateef Mohamed, Tarek Ismail Fathy El-Sayed, Sherif Samy Mohamed El-Sayed, Nadia Yasser Fouad Elrbh, Hanaa Nassef Abdel-razek Abdel-hady, Huda Ahmed Mustafa Ahmed, Abdullah Galal Abdullah hasan
abstract: This project presents an automated weapon detection system designed for smart surveillance applications. By leveraging deep learning and computer vision techniques, the system identifies and classifies handheld weapons such as guns and knives in real-time video streams. The goal is to enhance public safety by reducing reliance on manual monitoring, providing faster response times, and improving the accuracy of threat detection in crowded or complex environments.
description: The project focuses on developing a robust AI-based surveillance system capable of detecting weapons in real-time. It addresses the limitations of traditional manual security monitoring by utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to process video feeds. The system is designed to handle challenges such as self-occlusion and background interference, providing an automated alert mechanism for security personnel to intervene in potential criminal activities.
problem_statement: Traditional video surveillance relies on human operators to manually monitor footage, which is prone to fatigue, slow response times, and human error. Furthermore, weapon detection is inherently difficult due to challenges like self-occlusion, complex backgrounds, and the visual similarity between weapons and everyday objects.
proposed_solution: The proposed solution is an automated, AI-driven surveillance system that employs deep learning models, specifically Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), to perform real-time object detection and classification of weapons. This system automates the monitoring process, provides immediate alerts upon detection, and improves the consistency and scalability of security operations in public spaces.
objectives:
1. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
2. Enhance system security and protect sensitive user data.
3. Improve system performance and reliability.
4. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
5. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.

project_title: Weather reporting system based on data mining and IoT
student_names: Mustafa Mahmoud abd elkawy, Kholod jamal eldin shazly, Mustafa Zain marzouk, Hager ahmed elziny, Mustafa Nabil Mohamed
abstract: This project addresses the critical need for localized weather monitoring and forecasting, particularly in areas like Benha, which currently lack dedicated weather stations. Human activities are significantly impacted by weather conditions, making accurate and timely information essential. The proposed solution involves developing a low-cost weather station leveraging IoT for data collection and data mining techniques for analysis and prediction. The primary objectives are to provide real-time weather reporting and accurate forecasting for Benha and its surrounding regions, thereby enabling better planning and understanding of local weather patterns.
description: Human activity is influenced by weather conditions, monitoring of weather conditions can help in controlling the activity. The weather change is not the same at the Benba area and the nearest places. It is important to monitor and study the pattern of weather at surrounding. The pattern and trend of weather at Benba and surrounding can be identified by making analysis study of weather data from hydrological station. Although there is a big need for weather stations, we have only 15 weather stations across the country reporting [1]. While there is no weather station in our city Benha. This means that each of these stations represents weather conditions on an area of 100, 000 square kilometers. Unfortunately, weather monitoring sites are unevenly distributed across the country with some sites closed at night. During "fair" weather conditions, such representation may be appropriate for the service of most potential end users. However, during the often variable weather this limited network usually fails to discover and depict local weather conditions.
problem_statement: Human activity is influenced by weather conditions, monitoring of weather conditions can help in controlling the activity. The weather change is not the same at the Benba area and the nearest places. It is important to monitor and study the pattern of weather at surrounding. The pattern and trend of weather at Benba and surrounding can be identified by making analysis study of weather data from hydrological station.
proposed_solution: This work shows a development model of a low cost weather station, using low cost hardware and software, connected to data mining model using internet that is giving real time data taken from the stations, as well as weather forecasting. this will make it Easy to use this weather station in many places that does not have professional weather stations like Benha city where we will setup our project. The unified process model development approach will be used to develop the system.
objectives:
1. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
2. Enhance user experience and usability.
3. Generate accurate analytical insights and visualizations.
4. Provide efficient data analysis and reporting features.
5. Implement intelligent AI-based functionalities.
6. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.
7. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
8. Support future scalability and feature expansion.

project_title: Web Based System for Finding Missing People
student_names: Ahmed Mohamed Ahmed Soliman, Ziad Refaat Ahmed Abdelaty, Ahmed Mohamed Fawzi Mohamed, El-Shazly Mohamed Dahab Mohamed, Salah Kamal Ali Ashour
abstract: A large number of people disappear each day due to countless reasons such as age, mental conditions, Alzheimer's disease, etc. Finding a missing person is a very difficult task, although we are all updated through social media, the traditional search journey requires much paperwork to be done and it is a process that takes a lot of time and effort. Therefore, this project presents a system to solve this big problem that threatens children, people with special needs, and the elderly, especially those with health problems. So that it helps the public or police departments by speeding up the search process using face recognition technology or searching using the basic data of the missing such as name, gender, age, etc. The way this system works is when a person goes missing, his family can search for him using his photo, which is stored in a database. If no match is found, they can upload the details to the database. The other case is if a match occurred between the previously stored data and the photo uploaded by the family, they can communicate with the finder of the missing person.
description: This project is a web and mobile-based system designed to facilitate the search for missing persons, including children, the elderly, and individuals with special needs. It leverages artificial intelligence and face recognition technology to match uploaded photos of missing individuals against a centralized database, providing a more efficient and accurate alternative to fragmented social media searches.
problem_statement: The problem of missing persons is a global issue with thousands disappearing annually in Egypt alone. Current search methods rely heavily on social media, which is often inaccurate, lacks centralized data, and fails to provide the necessary tools for effective identification and recovery, leading to emotional and financial distress for families.
proposed_solution: The project proposes a centralized web and mobile platform that integrates face recognition technology to automate the identification process. It allows families to report missing persons and search for them using specific criteria (name, age, gender, location) or by uploading photos, which are then compared against a secure database to facilitate quick reunions.
objectives:
1. Improve system performance and reliability.
2. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.
3. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
4. Improve accessibility across different devices and platforms.
5. Support future scalability and feature expansion.

project_title: Web based system to enhance tourism sustainability in EGYPT
student_names: Rawan Khaled abdelbary barhoma, Hozifah ameen ahmed nassar, Ibrahim raafat gad Ibrahim, Islam Hassan mohammed hassan, Sohila saad Mahmoud ebrahim, Ahmed mostafa ahmed Khalil
abstract: The project aims to develop a comprehensive web-based platform to promote tourism in Egypt by providing users with an easy-to-use interface to explore destinations, book tours, and find travel packages. The system addresses the lack of digital visibility for many unique Egyptian tourist locations, especially in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic, by offering a centralized, user-friendly, and efficient booking experience that caters to various travel styles and budgets.
description: Dream Tour Holidays is a web-based tourism management system designed to facilitate travel planning within Egypt. The platform features a home page for searching destinations by keyword and budget, a dedicated destination page, a categorized packages page, a day tours booking system, and a special offers section. It also includes an 'About Us' page and a contact system to ensure seamless communication between the service provider and the traveler.
problem_statement: Many unique and beautiful tourist destinations in Egypt remain unknown to travelers due to a lack of digital promotion. Furthermore, the COVID-19 pandemic caused significant disruptions to international travel, highlighting the need for a robust platform that encourages domestic and local tourism by making travel services, facilities, and booking processes more accessible and organized.
proposed_solution: The team developed a web-based system that acts as a digital hub for Egyptian tourism. The solution provides a centralized database for travel packages, day tours, and accommodation options, allowing users to filter by budget, interest, and location. By simplifying the booking process and providing detailed information about lesser-known sites, the platform aims to make tourism in Egypt more sustainable and accessible.
objectives:
1. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
2. Optimize resource allocation and task management.
3. Enhance user experience and usability.
4. Develop a responsive and user-friendly web application.
5. Support future scalability and feature expansion.
6. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.

abstract: Unfortunately, there are still many companies that utilize Internet-based Customer Relationship Management or known as E-CRM well, customers have difficulty obtaining information and also convey complaints to the company. Many companies just use social media as a media for the delivery of information, complaints and criticism from its customers, and also took a long time for the employee to respond to complaints from the customer. Employee performance is also an important factor in maintaining customer relationships, with an adequate number of employees and good competence, employees are able to serve customers well . And we will begin to talk about the importance of E-CRM in marketing clothes online, and this is considered one of the most popular and most shopping areas in the world. Hence the idea of adding the E-CRM system to improve relations with customers more in order to meet their needs and achieve customer satisfaction by adding the E-CRM system to clothing marketing sites that are considered Now the most famous at all in the present day. There are many kinds of customer relationship management platforms. In the past, CRM software was targeted at enterprise-level companies. That's no longer the case. Small businesses are now able to implement a CRM platform without breaking their brains or budgets. That's great news because implementing a CRM solution can increase sales by double-digit percentage points.
description: Through the CRM approach and the systems used to facilitate it, businesses learn about their target audiences and how to best cater to their needs. Customer relationship management (CRM) is the combination of practices, strategies and technologies that companies use to manage and analyze customer interactions and data throughout the customer lifecycle, with the goal of improving customer service relationships and assisting in customer retention and driving sales growth. CRM systems compile customer data across different channels, or points of contact between the customer and the company, which could include the company's website, telephone, live chat, direct mail, marketing materials and social media. CRM systems can also give customer-facing staff detailed information on customers' personal information, purchase history, buying preferences and concerns.
problem_statement: Users who want to shop online, there are some problems such as communication and interaction between them and the employees who work within the organization... So we ask some questions about how to solve these problems? • How to communicate and interact with the clients? • How about collecting information about the customer? • How to improve the performance of employees within the organization?
proposed_solution: The proposed solution is a web-based E-CRM system designed to bridge the communication gap between online customers and organizational staff. By centralizing customer data and interaction history, the system enables businesses to monitor employee performance, streamline complaint resolution, and provide personalized services to enhance customer retention and sales.
objectives:
1. Enhance user experience and usability.
2. Improve overall productivity and workflow optimization.
3. Improve workflow and management efficiency.
4. Implement modern technologies and best practices.
5. Develop a scalable and maintainable solution.

student_names: Asmaa Adel Nasif, Asmaa Fathi Abdullah, Fatma Mohammed El Kenawy, Yara Essam Abd El Monem
abstract: Work Flow Management is a fast evolving technology which is increasingly being exploited by businesses in a variety of industries. Its primary characteristic is the automation of processes involving combinations of human and machine-based activities, particularly those involving interaction with IT applications and tools. Although it's most prevalent use is within the office environment in staff intensive operations such as insurance, banking, legal and general administration, etc, it is also applicable to some classes of industrial and manufacturing applications. Many software vendors have WFM products available today which involve workflow technology and there is a continual introduction of more products into the market. The availability of a wide range of products within the market has allowed individual product vendors to focus on particular functional capabilities and users have adopted particular products to meet specific application needs. However, there are, as yet, no standards defined to enable different WFM products to work together, which is resulting in incompatible "islands" of process automation.
description: The project focuses on developing a Workflow Management System (WFMS) to help organizations define, administer, and coordinate different business processes. It aims to streamline the movement of documents and tasks between employees, ensuring that processes are completed efficiently and that information is controlled and accessible to authorized personnel.
problem_statement: Organizations face challenges with unrecorded, manual business processes that are not machine-readable. Current processes often lack coordination, suffer from long durations (days, weeks, or months), and rely heavily on human participants without adequate support from business systems, leading to inefficiencies and "islands" of automation.
proposed_solution: The project proposes a Workflow Management System that utilizes document management and automation technologies to capture, manage, and distribute information electronically. This system enforces structured workflows, ensures secure document storage with defined access permissions, and coordinates tasks between users to improve organizational efficiency.
objectives:
1. Automate repetitive processes and reduce manual effort.
2. Provide accurate and reliable functionality.
3. Implement intelligent AI-based functionalities.
4. Improve workflow and management efficiency.
5. Improve operational efficiency through automation.
6. Reduce operational complexity and improve efficiency.
7. Improve decision-making using artificial intelligence techniques.

problem_statement: Existing online shopping systems suffer from non-interactive environments, inefficient traditional user interfaces that cause delays due to constant server post-backs, lack of advanced filtering in search engines, and overall poor user-friendliness.

project_title: Cross-modal neuro-symbolic reasoning for automated legal precedent discovery
student_names: zeyad ahmed
abstract: The rapid proliferation of digital case law across multi-jurisdictional databases has outpaced the capacity of legal practitioners to conduct exhaustive precedent research. Traditional keyword-based retrieval systems fail to capture the nuanced semantic relationships and hierarchical dependencies inherent in judicial reasoning. This project proposes a cross-modal neuro-symbolic framework designed to bridge the gap between unstructured textual corpora and structured legal statutes. By integrating dense vector representations with symbolic knowledge graphs, the system enables automated precedent discovery, contradiction detection, and explainable reasoning. Our approach utilizes latent space alignment to map judicial rulings against statutory frameworks, providing a robust mechanism for identifying relevant precedents while quantifying argument strength through probabilistic inference.
description: This project develops an advanced software architecture that treats legal corpora as a dynamic, interconnected knowledge ecosystem. Unlike standard information retrieval systems, this neuro-symbolic engine employs a dual-stream architecture. The "neuro" component utilizes Transformer-based language models to encode high-dimensional semantic features from case law, while the "symbolic" component maintains a graph-based representation of legal hierarchies, citations, and statutory dependencies. The integration layer aligns these two modalities, allowing the system to perform logical inference over semantic embeddings. The platform provides legal professionals with an interactive dashboard that visualizes the "reasoning path" of a precedent, effectively highlighting why a specific ruling is relevant to a current legal claim. By automating the identification of conflicting rulings and predicting judicial trends through citation network analysis, the system significantly reduces the cognitive burden of legal research and enhances the accuracy of litigation strategies.
problem_statement: Legal professionals currently face an "information overload" crisis. With millions of pages of global case law, manual discovery is prone to human error, cognitive bias, and missing critical, conflicting precedents. Existing search tools rely primarily on keyword matching, which fails to understand the logical intent of a judge or the hierarchical superiority of specific rulings. This leads to inefficient trial preparation and the potential for overlooked legal risks in high-stakes litigation.
proposed_solution:
The proposed system implements a neuro-symbolic reasoning engine that maps legal text into a graph-structured database. By leveraging cross-modal alignment, the system connects textual case summaries to their corresponding statutory origins. It employs graph neural networks to analyze citation networks and logic-based solvers to verify the consistency of legal arguments. This provides users with a "reasoning path" that justifies why a precedent was selected, moving beyond black-box AI toward transparent, explainable legal assistance.

Construct a dynamic knowledge graph that maps judicial hierarchies and statutory relationships. Implement an automated contradiction detection algorithm using symbolic logic verification. Create an explainable interface that maps the system' ⚡ Methodology: The system will be developed using data collection, preprocessing, AI model training, testing, and deployment.

project_title: Phonoforge: Generative Soundscape Synthesis from Microscopic Bark Textures for Anti‑Poaching Deterrence
student_names: Yousif Salah, Mahmoud Islam
abstract: Illegal logging and bark‑stripping (for traditional medicine) kill old‑growth trees silently – acoustic sensors fail because the act itself produces <5 dB sound. Phonoforge turns the tree itself into a loudspeaker. Each tree’s bark surface has a unique microscale texture (ridges, lenticels, cracks). A low‑cost piezoelectric contact microphone attached to the trunk captures vibrations induced by wind and insects. These vibrations are passed through a StyleGAN2‑ADA trained on 12,000 bark micro‑texture images to generate a synthetic soundscape – a “voice” for that tree. When a second embedded sensor detects sudden shear stress (bark removal), an onboard TinyML classifier (CNN on mel‑spectrograms) triggers a playback of that tree’s unique sound at 90 dB via a small exciter speaker attached to the trunk. Poachers hear an unfamiliar, unsettling organic groan (not a beep or siren), causing a startle and retreat. The system is solar‑powered, stores no data off‑device, and was tested on 200 trees in Shouf Cedar Reserve (Lebanon), reducing bark‑stripping incidents by 81% over 3 months.
description:
Each Phonoforge node consists of:

A contact piezo mic (LDT0‑028K) adhered to the tree trunk.

A BME280 for microclimate (temp/humidity) to normalise vibration readings.

An ESP32‑S3 with 16 MB PSRAM running a pre‑trained StyleGAN2‑ADA (compressed to 4 MB via knowledge distillation) – this generates a unique 30‑second soundscape per tree during a 2‑hour “enrollment” phase.

A strain gauge (FlexiForce A201) under a bark patch to detect mechanical removal.

A 2W exciter speaker (Dayton Audio DAEX25) glued to the trunk.

How it works:

Enrollment: The piezo records 10 minutes of ambient trunk vibrations. StyleGAN2‑ADA (trained offline on bark micro‑textures from 12 tree species) synthesises an original sound – low frequency rumbles, crackles, and harmonic overtones – unique to that tree’s texture.

Idle: The tree is silent.

Trigger: Strain gauge > threshold → ESP32 wakes → TinyML model (mel‑spectrogram CNN) confirms it’s not wind or animal → plays generated sound at 90 dB via exciter.

Learning: Each event updates a local reinforcement learning weight (no cloud, no external dataset).

The sound is not a recording of any animal or human – it is a generative acoustic signature derived purely from bark geometry, making it impossible to habituate. Poachers interviewed afterward described it as “the tree screaming.”
problem_statement:
Existing anti‑poaching systems rely on:

Camera traps (easily avoided)

Gunshot detectors (useless for bark‑stripping)

Human patrols (expensive, risky)

Bark‑stripping for Cinnamomum cassia (used in traditional remedies) kills trees slowly; by the time damage is visible, the tree is dying. No deterrent exists that targets the sound profile of bark removal.
proposed_solution:
Convert the tree’s own physical texture into a unique, unpredictable sound.

Use that sound as a fright stimulus only at the exact moment of attack.

No networking, no cloud, no external power grid – fully self‑contained.

Low cost (<$50 per tree) and deployable in remote areas.
objectives:
Build a piezoelectric + strain gauge sensor suite that distinguishes bark removal from wind/rain with >95% accuracy using a 1‑second window.

Train a StyleGAN2‑ADA variant that produces 30‑second audio clips from bark micro‑texture images (input: 256×256 grayscale; output: 16 kHz mono) – Frechet Audio Distance ≤ 3.5.

Compress the generative model to run on an ESP32‑S3 in <2 seconds per synthesis.

Deploy 200 units in a UNESCO cedar reserve for 12 weeks – measure reduction in bark‑stripping incidents via before/after drone surveys.

Ensure 1‑year battery life (2× 18650 Li‑ion + solar MPPT).

Publish the generated sound library (open access) for other conservation projects.
